questionText: Which singer's voice has been scientifically studied and found to use sub-harmonics?
funFact: Scientists at the University of Vienna found that Mercury used a vocal technique similar to Tibetan throat singing — no other rock singer has been documented doing this

questionText: Which country is Grimes from?
funFact: Grimes (Claire Boucher) taught herself to produce music using GarageBand and posted songs on MySpace — she later dated Elon Musk and named their child X Æ A-12

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Twenty One Pilots?
funFact: Twenty One Pilots is named after a moral dilemma in Arthur Miller's play 'All My Sons' — where a man must decide between saving 21 pilots or his own family business

questionText: Which David Lynch film features 'In Dreams' by Roy Orbison in a disturbing scene?
options: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me
funFact: Dennis Hopper lip-syncing to 'In Dreams' while terrorizing Kyle MacLachlan is one of cinema's most unsettling scenes — it completely changed how people hear Roy Orbison

questionText: Which TV show features 'Hannibal' by Brian Reitzell, featuring operatic vocals?
funFact: Hannibal's score is so disturbing that Brian Reitzell used sounds of bones cracking, meat tearing, and wine glasses shattering — all processed to sound beautiful

questionText: Which country is Susheela Raman from?
funFact: Susheela Raman bridges Tamil classical music with Western rock and electronic — she studied with master vocalists in India before recording in London and Paris

questionText: What was the first hip-hop song to be preserved in the Library of Congress?
correctAnswer: The Message by Grandmaster Flash & the Furious Five
options: The Message by Grandmaster Flash & the Furious Five
Fight the Power by Public Enemy
funFact: 'The Message' was one of the first hip-hop songs about poverty and urban decay — before this, rap was primarily party music

questionText: Who is the frontman of My Chemical Romance?
funFact: Gerard Way is also the creator of the 'Umbrella Academy' comic book series — the Netflix adaptation became a massive hit, making him a dual-threat creative

questionText: Which Spike Jonze film features Arcade Fire's 'Supersymmetry'?
funFact: Arcade Fire wrote 'Supersymmetry' while Win Butler's grandfather was dying — Spike Jonze heard it and said it perfectly captured the loneliness in his film about loving an AI

questionText: Which HBO series about drug trade features 'Way Down in the Hole' covered by different artists each season?
funFact: Each of The Wire's 5 seasons used a different version of the same Tom Waits song — from gospel to punk to kids singing, reflecting each season's perspective on Baltimore

questionText: Which country is Astor Piazzolla from?
funFact: Piazzolla revolutionized tango by adding jazz and classical elements — traditional tango musicians hated him so much that he received death threats

questionText: Which artist holds the record for the most #1 hits on the Billboard Hot 100?
funFact: The Beatles achieved 20 #1 hits in just 7 years (1964-1970) — an average of nearly 3 per year, a pace no artist has matched

questionText: Who founded The Velvet Underground with Lou Reed?
funFact: The Velvet Underground's debut album reportedly sold only 30,000 copies — but as Brian Eno said, 'everyone who bought one started a band'

questionText: Which Studio Ghibli film features Joe Hisaishi's most beloved piano theme?
funFact: Joe Hisaishi has scored every Hayao Miyazaki film since 1984 — their partnership is the Japanese equivalent of Spielberg and John Williams

questionText: Which TV series about advertising in the 1960s features a score by David Carbonara?
funFact: Mad Men's opening uses 'A Beautiful Mine' by RJD2 — the silhouette of a man falling through advertising images became one of TV's most iconic opening sequences

questionText: Which country is Susekt from?
funFact: Suspekt is one of Denmark's most successful and controversial rap groups — their explicit lyrics and wild live shows have made them a cultural phenomenon since the early 2000s

questionText: What record did Adele break with her album '25'?
correctAnswer: 3.38 million copies sold in the first week in the US
options: 3.38 million copies sold in the first week in the US
First album to go diamond in a day
funFact: Adele broke *NSYNC's 15-year-old first-week sales record — in an era when physical album sales were supposedly dead. She also refused to put '25' on streaming initially

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Gasolin'?
funFact: Kim Larsen went on to have an even bigger solo career after Gasolin' — his song 'Papirsklip' is considered Denmark's unofficial national anthem

questionText: Which film features 'Nightcall' by Kavinsky?
funFact: Kavinsky is a French electronic artist who invented a backstory where he's a zombie who died in a car crash in 1986 — 'Nightcall' perfectly captured Drive's neon-noir atmosphere

questionText: Which TV comedy features 'Unpiped' and a very short, catchy bass riff?
funFact: 'Frolic' by Italian composer Luciano Michelini was written in 1974 for an Italian film — Larry David discovered it 26 years later and made it the most recognizable comedy theme of the 2000s

questionText: Which country is D-A-D from?
funFact: D-A-D were originally called 'Disneyland After Dark' until Disney's lawyers came calling — they became one of Scandinavia's biggest hard rock bands despite the name change

questionText: Which composer wrote the 'Moonlight Sonata'?
funFact: 'Moonlight Sonata' wasn't Beethoven's name — a critic called it 'moonlight' after Beethoven's death. Beethoven himself just called it 'Sonata quasi una fantasia'

questionText: Who is the lead vocalist of Panic! at the Disco?
funFact: Brendon Urie became the ONLY member of Panic! at the Disco — all other original members left, but he continued as a one-man band until officially disbanding in 2023

questionText: Which Bond film features Billie Eilish's 'No Time to Die'?
funFact: Billie Eilish was 17 when she recorded 'No Time to Die' with her brother Finneas — she's the youngest artist ever to write and record a Bond theme

questionText: Which Apple TV+ series features a score inspired by 1970s synth?
funFact: Severance's score uses vintage Moog synthesizers and reverse recordings — it creates a sterile, unsettling corporate feeling that perfectly matches the show's dystopian office setting

questionText: Which country is Laid Back from?
funFact: Laid Back's 'Sunshine Reggae' and 'Bakerman' were huge international hits in the 1980s — two Danes making reggae-influenced synth-pop that topped charts across Europe

questionText: Which Danish jazz bassist is considered one of the greatest in history?
funFact: NHØP played with Oscar Peterson, Dexter Gordon, and almost every jazz great of the 20th century — he was offered a spot in the Count Basie Orchestra at age 17 but his mother said he was too young

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Imagine Dragons?
funFact: 'Radioactive' held the record for longest stay on the Billboard Hot 100 at 87 weeks — it spent nearly two years on the chart

questionText: Which Tarantino film features 'Cat People (Putting Out Fire)' by David Bowie?
options: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
funFact: Tarantino used the Giorgio Moroder version of 'Cat People' for one of cinema's most tense scenes — Shosanna preparing for revenge, applying makeup like war paint

questionText: Which TV series about a high school chemistry teacher features Dave Porter's score?
funFact: Dave Porter used sounds of meth-making equipment (beakers, tubes, burners) as percussion elements in the Breaking Bad score — the music literally sounds like cooking

questionText: Which country is Mercyful Fate from?
funFact: King Diamond's falsetto and face paint inspired an entire generation of black metal musicians in Norway — without Danish Mercyful Fate, Norwegian black metal might not exist

questionText: What did Radiohead famously do with the release of 'In Rainbows'?
correctAnswer: Let fans pay whatever they wanted
options: Let fans pay whatever they wanted
Gave it away on USB drives
funFact: The 'pay what you want' experiment was revolutionary in 2007 — most fans paid between £4-8, and the album still debuted at #1 on traditional charts when the physical release followed

questionText: Which film features 'Stuck in the Middle with You' by Stealers Wheel during an ear-cutting scene?
funFact: Stealers Wheel were a Scottish folk-pop duo — they never imagined their cheerful song would become synonymous with one of cinema's most violent scenes
backgroundSongName: Stuck in the Middle with You

questionText: Which TV series about the British royal family features 'Crown' by Hans Zimmer?
funFact: Hans Zimmer composed the Crown theme in his signature minimalist style — the piano melody has become as recognizable as the show's cast
